design_philosophy.core_loop: Rail-based helicopter shooting with dynamic perspective shifts—third-person behind-view for navigation and ground targets, top-down view for boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision aiming and dodging while maintaining forward momentum through scripted flight paths; perspective switching creates cognitive shifts between spatial navigation types
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting an armed military helicopter to eliminate enemy forces across varied combat zones
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits fully to perspective duality as core mechanic rather than consistency; arcade-to-console port philosophy preserves arcade structure at cost of technical optimization, prioritizing gameplay fidelity over frame stability

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; predetermined rail path constrains movement options—difficulty emerges from enemy placement patterns and dodge timing requirements rather than player choice

progression.upgrade_system: Guns and missiles available as base weapons; no documented persistent upgrades
replayability_hooks: Stage-specific enemy patterns reward pattern recognition across replays
Perspective switching mechanic creates distinct skill challenges per stage phase

world_building_techniques: Implicit conflict framing via enemy type (guerrilla forces referenced in setup)

boss_design.philosophy: Perspective inversion strategy—boss battles shift to top-down vertical scrolling shoot-'em-up format, creating distinct mechanical breakpoints within each stage

faction_indicators: Enemy guerrilla forces (framed as hostile combatants)
danger_signals: Vehicle-based combat against ground and air targets
environmental_storytelling: Varied deployment zones suggest global military operations
Enemy vehicle types (helicopters, tanks, ships) imply escalating threat levels
